mission.title: Install and Configure IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service (IKS)
mission.description: IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service (IKS) is a managed Kubernetes offering that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on IBM Cloud. It is ideal for users looking for a fully managed solution with integrated IBM Cloud services. Key trade-offs include reliance on IBM Cloud for management and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the IBM Cloud CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the IBM Cloud CLI, which is necessary for interacting with IBM Cloud services.

```bash

curl -sL https://ibm.biz/idt-installer | bash

```

> This command downloads and installs the IBM Cloud CLI.

steps.title: Login to IBM Cloud
steps.description:
Authenticate your IBM Cloud account to manage resources.

```bash

ibmcloud login

```

> Follow the prompts to enter your IBM Cloud credentials.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version, node count, and region.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-create --name my-cluster --zone us-south-1 --num-workers 2 --kube-version 1.30

```

> Adjust the zone and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your local kubeconfig file to interact with the new cluster.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-config --cluster my-cluster

```

> This command sets the kubeconfig context to your new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see your nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up additional features like autoscaling and monitoring.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-update --cluster my-cluster --enable-autoscale

ibmcloud ks cluster-monitoring-enable --cluster my-cluster

```

> Enables autoscaling for your cluster.
> Enables monitoring for your cluster.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command will remove the cluster and all associated resources. Data loss may occur.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-delete --cluster my-cluster --force

```

> Use --force to bypass confirmation prompts.

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs.

```bash

ibmcloud resource service-instance-delete <service-instance-name>

```

> Replace <service-instance-name> with your actual service instance name.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Backup your cluster, upgrade to the new version, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-backup --cluster my-cluster

ibmcloud ks cluster-update --cluster my-cluster --kube-version 1.30

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup your cluster before upgrading.
> Upgrade to the specified Kubernetes version.
> Verify that all nodes are in 'Ready' state after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized' when running kubectl commands
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the kubeconfig is not set up correctly.

**Fix:**
Run 'ibmcloud ks cluster-config --cluster my-cluster' to set the correct context.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources' when creating a cluster
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the requested resources exceed the available quota.

**Fix:**
Check your IBM Cloud resource quotas and adjust the cluster specifications.

troubleshooting.title: Nodes are in 'NotReady' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the nodes are not properly initialized or have network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the node logs with 'kubectl describe nodes' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cluster not found' when trying to access the cluster
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the cluster name is incorrect or if you are not logged in.

**Fix:**
Ensure you are logged in and the cluster name is correct.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.30 features including improved scheduling and resource management.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy API versions for certain resources.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Ensure all resources are updated to use the latest API versions.

resolution.summary: You should see the nodes listed with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the IBM Cloud CLI and kubectl installed and configured.
